blockingIssues.description: update() accepts non-object JSON patches, which can change _data into non-object values; subsequent materialization assumes object and can panic via unreachable! in read_current_doc() path.

iso8601_to_epoch_ms does not validate calendar-correct dates (month/day and leap-year combinations), so invalid dates (e.g., 2024-02-31) are accepted and normalized, leading to incorrect query_at/query_between behavior.

appliedUpdates.description: Updated .factory/library/architecture.md to remove stale temp-table references and align _cairn_tx_context lifecycle text with the regular-system-table protocol.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a mission-authoring guardrail: expectedBehavior statements in features.json must align with validation-contract assertions and trigger model semantics (e.g., BEFORE UPDATE/DELETE means no INSERT history rows).
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: operations feature time-travel-queries-and-integration still expects lifecycle/query_all counts implying INSERT history rows, while validation-contract and implementation use mutation-only history.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Tighten rust-worker process enforcement by requiring explicit transcript evidence of RED-before-GREEN and flagging deviations in handoff.skillFeedback when edits precede failing test additions.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Reviewer observed the time-travel-queries-and-integration transcript showing code edits before test additions while skill feedback still marked procedure as followed.

rejectedObservations.observation: Architecture docs are internally contradictory about _cairn_tx_context temp vs regular table.
rejectedObservations.reason: duplicate observation across two reviews; applied once via direct library update.
